stories.headline: Mahomes' Busted Wheel Is Healed, and the King Rides Into Camp
stories.read: Big news off the Kansas City wire, pardner: Patrick Mahomes tore his knee wide open back in Week 15, went under the knife in December, and the whole saloon's been frettin' he'd start the year laid up. Well quit your frettin', the docs are fixin' to hand him a full clean bill this very week, he saddles up Friday, and he's aimin' to ride Week 1 with no reins holdin' him back. The herd's been shovin' him down the board on injury jitters, and that, pardner, is pure fool's gold. Grab that golden arm at a spooked-down price before the rest of the outfit figures out the King's healthy.

stories.headline: DJ Moore Rides North to Buffalo, and Luther Burden Strikes Gold in Chicago
stories.read: The Bears shipped DJ Moore clear up to Buffalo, pardner, and that leaves the top receiver's chair in Chicago wide open for young Luther Burden to plant himself in. The kid was quiet as a rookie but slick as they come on every route he ran, and now the throws come pourin' his way to match the talent. That's cheap gold, a diamond in the rough the saloon's still sleepin' on, so stake your claim before the herd wakes up. And out in Buffalo, Josh Allen just saddled a proper gun, so his stock climbs right along with him.

stories.headline: Panthers Break Camp Today, and the Backfield's a Two-Horse Race
stories.read: Carolina threw open the corral gates today, first outfit in all the land to break camp, and their backfield's shapin' up a two-horse race. Chuba Hubbard's back as the lead horse, healthy and fixin' to carry the mail, so he's a fine pick, just don't pay top dollar expectin' every last carry. The fella to watch is Jonathon Brooks, healed up clean from two busted wheels, fast with a cut that's already turnin' heads, and he's the spare horse liable to steal the reins by midseason. Stash him cheap and late, pardner, that's how you strike a vein nobody else has touched.
